attacking_style: Rarely attacks conventionally; instead deploys sustained time trial power on steep finishes (2-3km to go) or bridges solo to breakaways using pursuit power (45s gaps on climbs). Leverages TT engine rather than explosive accelerations.
team_tactics: When leading GC, team conserves resources and avoids pulling, relying on Dennis's ITT advantage to regain time. Often deployed as elite domestique for GC leaders, capable of controlling 40-50km finales and gaining minutes on mountain descents through pacing power.
terrain_preference: Dominates technical time trials with corners and descents over pure power courses. Excels in short prologues (8-20km) and can survive selective punchy circuits with team support. Struggles on extended steep climbs (2km+) when pure climbers accelerate.
vulnerability: Catastrophic fade on climbs after strong starts when gradient steepens beyond 2-3km. Gets dropped when pace lifts on sustained steep gradients. Crashes and mechanicals have derailed promising positions multiple times. TT strength doesn't translate to extended climbing efforts.
key_patterns: Progressive pacing: enhances time gaps throughout TT courses rather than fading, showing superior execution (4+ observations)
Technical superiority: maximizes gains on corners/descents in TTs, not pure power sections (3 observations)
Bonus hunting: aggressively contests intermediate sprints for seconds when targeting GC (3 observations)
Domestique elite: world-class pacing work gains 2+ minutes on descents, controls 40-50km finales (4 observations)
Steep climb weakness: cracks when gradients steepen beyond 2-3km despite TT strength (5+ observations)
TTT anchor: consistently drives final kilometers to secure team time trial victories (3 observations)
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 showed world-class pursuit power bridging solo for victory, but crash the next day indicates inconsistency. Earlier 2022-2023 results mixed with strong ITT performances but climbing vulnerabilities persist.
